document_id: Kimberly Park II, LLC, et al

findings.description: The original PDF header layout places STATE OF NORTH CAROLINA / COUNTY OF WAKE on the left and BEFORE THE PROPERTY TAX COMMISSION / SITTING AS THE / STATE BOARD OF EQUALIZATION AND REVIEW on the right in a side-by-side arrangement. The HTML renders this as a table, which is acceptable but visually different from the original centered/side-by-side text layout.
findings.evidence: PDF: Left-right text arrangement. HTML: Rendered as a structured table with cell borders.

findings.description: The official seal/stamp of the North Carolina Property Tax Commission and the Chairman's signature are missing from the HTML rendering.
findings.evidence: PDF page 6 shows the Commission seal image and Robert C. Hunter's handwritten signature. HTML page 6 shows only text 'Robert C. Hunter, Chairman' without the seal or signature image.

findings.description: The ATTEST signature of Stephen W. Pelfrey is missing from the HTML rendering.
findings.evidence: PDF page 6 shows a handwritten signature above 'Stephen W. Pelfrey, Commission Secretary'. HTML shows only the text.

findings.description: The word 'Comission' appears in both PDF and HTML - this is a typo in the original preserved in the HTML, which is correct behavior. No issue here, but noting that on page 6 the signature block layout differs - the HTML renders it as a table rather than free-form text.
findings.evidence: PDF: Free-form signature block layout. HTML: Table-based layout for signature block.

findings.description: The income approach table on page 5 is correctly reproduced in the HTML with all data intact, though styling differs slightly.
findings.evidence: PDF table has $ signs in cells with dollar-sign column alignment. HTML shows values like '$1,309,667' as formatted text. All values match.

findings.description: Multiple h1 headings used. 'FINAL DECISION' is the first h1, but other major section headings also appear as h2, which is acceptable. However, the document has nested <main> elements, which is structurally invalid HTML but not a content fidelity issue.
findings.evidence:
The HTML has

FINAL DECISION

as the primary h1, which matches the PDF. The heading hierarchy h1 -> h2 is consistent.

findings.description: Each page of the PDF is embedded as a full-page image AND also transcribed as text/HTML content. This means all content appears twice in the HTML — once as an image and once as structured text.
findings.evidence: Six

findings.description: Alt text for images is generic and not descriptive of actual content. All six images have nearly identical alt text patterns.
findings.evidence: Alt texts are 'Page 1 of the Final Decision document', 'Page 2 of the Final Decision document', etc. These are generic identifiers rather than descriptive of the content shown.

findings.description: The case identification table at the top of page 1 is rendered as a table in the HTML, which is a reasonable structural interpretation of the layout. The data values are all accurate.
findings.evidence: All case numbers (18 PTC 0029, 18 PTC 0025, 18 PTC 0026) and party names match the PDF. The financial data table on page 5 is also accurately reproduced with correct values.

findings.description: The PDF refers to 'Comission Member Penny' (with a typo — single 'm'), and the HTML preserves this typo, which is correct fidelity behavior.
findings.evidence: PDF page 6: 'Comission Member Penny dissents without separate opinion.' HTML: 'Comission Member Penny dissents without separate opinion.'
